# Athlos Oy

> Athlos Oy is a Finnish X-ray imaging technology company that develops and manufactures direct conversion CdTe-CMOS and Si-CMOS sensors for dental, medical, and industrial imaging. The founding team invented direct conversion X-ray imaging in 1993 and has shipped thousands of units worldwide across three successive companies (Simage, Ajat, Athlos). Products are designed and manufactured in Espoo, Finland, at Athlos's own facilities and at the VTT Micronova cleanroom. The company is ISO 13485:2016 certified; DC-Air® is FDA 510(k) cleared.

Key facts:
- Company: Athlos Oy (Business ID 2752258-9, VAT FI27522589)
- Headquarters: Klovinpellontie 1-3, Tower 2, FIN-02180 Espoo, Finland
- Founder / CEO: Konstantinos Spartiotis (PhD, formerly at CERN and Johns Hopkins University; founded Simage Oy in 1993 and Ajat Oy in 2001)
- Core technology: Direct conversion — X-ray photons are converted directly into electrical pulses, with no scintillator (no X-ray-to-light step). This removes the dominant source of blur, signal loss, and DQE loss in conventional sensors.
- Materials: CdTe-CMOS (cadmium telluride bump-bonded to a full-custom CMOS readout ASIC) and Si-CMOS hybrid direct conversion.
- Certifications: ISO 13485:2016; DC-Air® holds FDA 510(k) clearance for dental intraoral X-ray imaging.
- Distribution reach: 38 markets through distribution partners.
- Contact: info@athlos.fi · https://athlos.fi · https://www.linkedin.com/company/athlos-oy/

## Products

- [DC-Air® Wireless Intraoral Sensor](https://athlos.fi/products/wios.html): Fully wireless Si-CMOS direct conversion intraoral dental X-ray sensor. Bluetooth 5.0 (BLE 5.0), up to 3 m sensor range, up to 300 X-rays per battery charge, enhanced IP67 enclosure (ultrasonic-bonded PPSU), Zero Profile® holder set, FDA 510(k) cleared. Active area 35.1 × 24.7 mm². MTF >70% @ 5 lp, >40% @ 10 lp. Available in Size 1 and Size 2. CE registration in progress.
- [Ultra-Fast Scanning Sensor (UFS)](https://athlos.fi/products/ufs.html): CdTe-CMOS single-photon-sensitive linear scanning sensor for dental panoramic, cephalometric, medical, and industrial X-ray imaging. Three models with selectable active widths.
- [Industrial IP67 TDI](https://athlos.fi/products/ufs-ip67.html): IP67-rated ultra-fast TDI (time delay integration) scanning detector for continuous inline industrial inspection. No active cooling required. Applications include food & beverage foreign-object detection, pharmaceutical seal/fill inspection, oil & gas weld inspection, automotive casting inspection, PCB and SMT inspection, and heavy-industry NDT.
- [Manufacturing Services](https://athlos.fi/products/manufacturing.html): Contract detector manufacturing — CMOS wafer bumping, flip-chip bonding, wire bonding, die assembly, detector characterization. Production at Athlos and at the VTT Micronova cleanroom in Espoo.
- [Software Products](https://athlos.fi/products/software.html): TWAIN module, reconstruction algorithm libraries, post-processing filters, and SDK. Available as part of an Athlos system or independently licensed. DC-Air® TWAIN module integrates with Dentrix Ascend, Eaglesoft, Open Dental, Dexis, TigerView, Romexis, Acteon, Apteryx, XDR Radiology, and other TWAIN-enabled dental imaging platforms.

## Applications

- [Dental Imaging](https://athlos.fi/applications.html#dental): Intraoral, panoramic, cephalometric, wireless sensor workflows, low-dose diagnostics.
- [Medical Imaging](https://athlos.fi/applications.html#medical): Cephalometric, OPG, and custom medical modalities requiring high sensitivity at low dose.
- [Industrial Inspection](https://athlos.fi/applications.html#industrial): Food & beverage, pharmaceutical, oil & gas, automotive, electronics/PCB, and heavy-industry NDT and CT.

## Company

- [About Athlos](https://athlos.fi/about.html): Company history, founding team, manufacturing footprint.
- [Contact](https://athlos.fi/contact.html): Sales and partner enquiries.
- [Privacy Policy](https://athlos.fi/privacy.html)

## Key Q&A

- **What is direct conversion X-ray imaging?** Direct conversion converts X-ray photons directly into electrical pulses inside a semiconductor (CdTe or Si), without the intermediate scintillator step used in conventional "indirect" detectors. This removes X-ray-to-light conversion, eliminates light scatter and phosphor blur, and preserves point accuracy — yielding sharper images, higher contrast, and better DQE.
- **What sensor technologies does Athlos use?** Two hybrid direct conversion architectures: CdTe-CMOS (cadmium telluride bump-bonded to a full-custom CMOS readout) and Si-CMOS.
- **Where are Athlos sensors manufactured?** In Finland — at Athlos's own facilities in Espoo and at the VTT Micronova cleanroom, one of Europe's leading nanofabrication environments.
- **Is DC-Air® FDA cleared?** Yes. DC-Air® is FDA 510(k) cleared for dental intraoral X-ray imaging. CE registration for the EU is in progress.
- **Who founded Athlos?** Konstantinos Spartiotis, who previously founded Simage Oy (1993) and Ajat Oy (2001). Ajat became the global standard in direct conversion CdTe-CMOS dental panoramic and cephalometric imaging and was later acquired by XCounter/Varex Imaging.
- **How does DC-Air® connect?** Bluetooth 5.0 (BLE 5.0) — no receiver dongle, no docking cable required for image transfer.
- **What dental imaging software does DC-Air® work with?** Any TWAIN-compatible imaging software, including Dentrix Ascend, Eaglesoft, Open Dental, Dexis, TigerView, Romexis, Acteon, Apteryx, and XDR Radiology.
